hard.next_step: 6/24/26 GK: IDB shared positive message, but based on internal agendas/team vacations the decision update will be early to mid-July
hard.ais_why: No Avoma calls exist across opp, account, and idbinvest.org attendee searches, so no buyer-stated AI evidence exists. This is a partner-led (BCT) CLM cycle following a standard RFP with iContract plus Merlin in scope and no AI rating captured, the canonical AI Resistant profile.

north_star_verdict.headline: IDB named Zycus one of two finalists and sent a positive signal on 24 Jun 2026, but the decision has slipped to early-to-mid July, IDB's MSA feedback has been outstanding since 24 Apr, and every buyer touch runs through partner BCT with no direct Zycus-to-IDB relationship.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 10 Nov 2025, so ~8 months in cycle and ~7 months in and around Shortlisted. Close date 14 Jul 2026 is 8 days out. Last activity 1 Jul 2026 is an internal rep task (add contact roles), not a two-way buyer touch. MSA completed with BCT 24 Apr 2026, IDB feedback still outstanding ~73 days later. Even if the award lands early-to-mid July, MSA redlines, signature and any PO cannot credibly complete by 14 Jul, and the close date has already slipped six times since April.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Best Case at most until IDB returns MSA feedback and confirms the award in writing; Upside Key Deal overstates certainty given no engaged economic buyer, no confirmed champion, an unknown second finalist, and a fully partner-mediated pulse.

metrics.narrative: No quantified value case is recorded anywhere in the Salesforce log, and there are no Avoma calls to mine for buyer-stated impact (zero calls across the opportunity, account, and attendee-email pulls). The product scope is iContract plus Merlin (source: Products field), implying a contract-lifecycle-management consolidation for a development finance institution, but no cost, contract-cycle-time, compliance, or savings numbers were ever articulated by IDB in any captured source. Because BCT runs all buyer access, the single move is to have BCT extract IDB's business case (contract turnaround, audit exposure, savings) in writing before the early-to-mid-July decision so the value story can anchor to the economic buyer.

sources: Account contact list (prefetch): Orlando Ferreira (CFO), Julio Cesar Lima (MD, IT&A)
economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified or engaged after roughly 8 months on a $100k deal, and there are zero opportunity contact roles on record (a rep task on 1 Jul 2026 was pushed specifically to add IDB buyer contacts, confirming none exist yet). Account-level contacts include a CFO, Orlando Ferreira, and an IT Managing Director, Julio Cesar Lima, but neither is proven to be involved in this opportunity's scope and there is no call or note where either showed budget authority. Because BCT mediates every buyer touch, Zycus cannot validate who signs; mapping and reaching the economic buyer through BCT, ideally a direct introduction to Orlando Ferreira, is a ranked move before contracting closes.

decision_criteria.narrative: The criteria are inferred from the procurement motion, not documented from IDB's own words. IDB ran a formal RFP (received 10 Nov 2025, submitted by Zycus 24 Nov 2025), down-selected to finalists on 30 Dec 2025, held a demo and PoC with the BCT team on 15 Jan 2026, ran an on-site session on 2 Mar 2026, refined user definitions and user-count-based pricing, and required a PwC reference call completed around 18 May 2026. That sequence shows weighting on functional CLM fit (iContract), user-count commercials, and third-party proof, but the specific scored requirements were never captured in Salesforce and no call exists to confirm them. Confirm the formal evaluation scorecard through BCT.

decision_process.narrative: The process is reconstructable from the Next Step log: RFP, down-selection to two finalists (30 Dec 2025), demo/PoC (15 Jan 2026), on-site session (2 Mar 2026), pricing and BAFO (best and final offer sent ~24 Mar 2026), MSA completion with BCT (24 Apr 2026), a PwC reference call (~18 May 2026), and a final vendor decision now expected early-to-mid July 2026. IDB is running a structured two-finalist selection driven entirely through partner BCT. The 24 Jun 2026 note attributes the latest slip to 'internal agendas/team vacations', a credible but un-forcing reason. The gap is that Zycus sees this process only second-hand through BCT, with no direct line to the IDB decision committee or a named approval chain.

sources: Next_Step__c 4/24/26 (MSA completed), 5/5/26 ('quiet from BCT; Harshad fu with BCT for feedback on MSA'), 12/15/25 (representations and warranties)
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are partially visible: the MSA was completed with BCT on 24 Apr 2026 and sent to IDB, with IDB feedback still outstanding as of 1 Jul 2026 (~73 days). Earlier, on 15 Dec 2025, the partner requested help with representations and warranties, showing legal terms were already in play. The forcing function is the 14 Jul 2026 close, which is not credible if MSA feedback, redlines and signature must still happen. As a U.S. buyer a PO is likely not required, so a missing PO is not a gap here. Visibility into IDB legal and procurement is blocked by the partner channel; getting BCT to surface the specific MSA objections would create the missing clarity.

identify_pain.narrative: No explicit pain statement from IDB is captured in any source, and there are no Avoma calls to attribute pain to a named buyer. The inferred pain is contract-lifecycle-management modernization at a development finance institution, consistent with the iContract scope and a formal RFP, but the operational or financial driver IDB itself articulated (compliance exposure, contract turnaround time, audit risk) is unknown, and the pain owner is unconfirmed. Surfacing IDB's stated driver through BCT is required to anchor the value story for the economic buyer.

sources: Next_Step__c 6/15/26 ('check in with Mariana on IDB updates'), 5/11/26 (Digesh/PwC reference)
champion.narrative: No buyer-side champion is confirmed. A person named 'Mariana' appears in the 15 Jun 2026 note as the contact Grace Kim planned to check in with for IDB updates, but her role, title, and advocacy are unconfirmed and she may be a BCT-side or coordination contact rather than an IDB sponsor. Digesh from PwC is an external reference-providing ally, not an internal IDB champion. After 8 months Zycus has no proven internal advocate driving urgency inside IDB, which is the single biggest structural weakness in a two-vendor race run through a partner. Confirming Mariana's role and converting her or another IDB contact into an inside advocate is the top non-commercial move.

sources: Next_Step__c 5/11/26 ('IDB notified us as 1 of 2 finalists')
competition.narrative: IDB explicitly told Zycus it is one of two finalists (11 May 2026 note), so there is exactly one competing finalist, but that competitor is unnamed in every source and Zycus has gathered no competitive intelligence on it across the entire cycle. The current-state or incumbent CLM at IDB is also unrecorded. Zycus is competing partially blind: without knowing the rival, the wedge cannot be tailored. The wedge that does exist is the iContract plus Merlin AI-enabled CLM story and a completed PwC reference; the move is to have BCT surface who the other finalist is and what IDB values in each, then sharpen the Merlin and iContract differentiation accordingly.

deal_movement.summary: The pulse is COOLING and fully partner-mediated. Real forward motion happened Nov 2025-May 2026 (RFP, downselect, demo, on-site, BAFO, MSA, reference), but since the MSA went to IDB on 24 Apr the deal has waited on IDB feedback, with the close date slipped six times and the decision now pushed to early-to-mid July. The 24 Jun positive message is the only recent buyer signal and it arrived through BCT.

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is one of two finalists in IDB's CLM procurement, confirmed 11 May 2026 and still active with no decision logged. The other finalist is unnamed and unknown, and no competitive intelligence was captured during the 8-month cycle, so threat level is held high on lack of visibility rather than a specific rival signal. The strongest current risk is not the named rival but the blind spot itself plus IDB's silence: with the deal run entirely through BCT and no direct relationship, Zycus cannot see how the two solutions are being compared. The move is to have BCT name the other finalist and IDB's comparison criteria before the mid-July decision, then anchor the Merlin AI contract-intelligence and iContract platform story as the wedge.

best_practice_check.summary: This is a partner-mediated, two-finalist CLM deal 8 days from close with no direct buyer relationship. The win levers now are breaking the partner-only dependency, naming the rival, and converting the 24 Jun positive signal into a written award.
flags: Break the BCT-only dependency: get a direct line to the IDB decision committee (start with CFO Orlando Ferreira) before the mid-July award.
Have BCT name the other finalist and IDB's comparison criteria so the Merlin and iContract wedge can be tailored.
Convert the 24 Jun positive message into a written award confirmation plus an MSA-to-signature timeline before the 14 Jul date slips again.

champion_strength.summary: No confirmed internal IDB champion after 8-plus months. Mariana is the only named IDB-side touchpoint and her role and advocacy are unverified; PwC's Digesh is an external reference ally, not a champion. In a two-finalist race run entirely through BCT, the absence of a direct internal advocate is the deal's biggest structural weakness.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: No AI narrative has been positioned to IDB. The scope is iContract plus Merlin, but with zero buyer calls there is no evidence Merlin's AI contract-intelligence story was landed; AIS is Negative (2.0) and this reads as a functional, partner-run CLM cycle.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score__c 2.0, AIS_Status__c Negative. No Avoma calls exist to test whether the field under- or over-rates buyer AI appetite; the record shows a partner-led CLM RFP with no AI champion, POC, or budget evidence, the canonical AI Resistant profile.

items.detail: Close date has slipped six times since April (latest to 14 Jul); with the decision only expected early-to-mid July, MSA redlines and signature cannot credibly complete by the forecast date.
Amount was cut from $185k to $100k on 16 Apr 2026, a defensive scope-shrink signal that usually means the buyer is trimming cost or phasing the implementation.
No economic buyer or champion is engaged on the opportunity; CFO Orlando Ferreira and MD Julio Cesar Lima sit at account level only and have not been reached through BCT.
The second finalist is unnamed after the full cycle, so Zycus is positioning partially blind and cannot counter the rival's fit or pricing.

confidence_signals.summary: The pulse is cooling and fully partner-mediated. The 24 Jun positive message is encouraging but second-hand, the decision keeps slipping, and there is no direct buyer engagement or contact role to corroborate intent.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is one of two finalists at IDB Invest with a 24 Jun positive signal and a decision due early-to-mid July, but the whole deal runs through partner BCT with no direct IDB relationship, no engaged economic buyer, an unnamed rival, and the amount already cut from $185k to $100k.

win_position.text: Holds mid-band, not a confident lead: finalist status (1 of 2, per 11 May note) plus a 24 Jun positive message earn it, but no direct IDB relationship, no engaged economic buyer, and an unknown rival cap it below Vendor Selected confidence.
The second finalist is still unnamed after 8 months, so Zycus is positioning partially blind against a rival it cannot see (source: Next Step 11 May 2026).
The amount was cut from $185k to $100k on 16 Apr, a defensive scope-shrink that usually signals the buyer is trimming cost or phasing rather than committing fully.

deal_momentum.text: The deal reached contracting mechanics: BAFO sent ~24 Mar and MSA completed with BCT on 24 Apr, showing real commercial and legal motion this year.
IDB's MSA feedback has sat outstanding ~73 days and the close date has slipped six times since April, so momentum is now stalled on the buyer's side.

customer_commitment.text: IDB drove buyer-led proof steps: it named Zycus 1 of 2 finalists (11 May) and completed a PwC reference call (~18 May).
There is no signed award; the 24 Jun 'positive message' is second-hand via BCT with the decision pushed out on internal vacations, so commitment is unconfirmed.

deal_risk.text: Single-channel dependency: every buyer touch is filtered through partner BCT with zero Zycus-to-IDB contact roles, so Zycus cannot verify the award or the signatory.
No economic buyer or champion is engaged; CFO Orlando Ferreira and MD Julio Cesar Lima sit at account level only, and the 14 Jul close is not credible against an early-to-mid-July decision.
